target_goal.label: independent RedCube visual-deliverable domain agent with an OPL Runtime Manager federation bridge over external Hermes-Agent
target_goal.summary: Freeze the final target shape in which direct RedCube entry and OPL Runtime Manager-federated entry converge onto the same service-safe RedCube domain-agent entry, while OPL Runtime Manager is the thin product-managed adapter/projection layer over external Hermes-Agent substrate, RedCube owns visual-domain truth, and Codex CLI remains the default concrete executor.

final_target_route.redcube_direct_entry: User -> RedCube Product Entry -> RedCube service-safe domain entry -> executor adapter -> concrete executor -> RedCube visual-domain truth surfaces
final_target_route.opl_federated_entry: User -> OPL Product Entry -> OPL Runtime Manager -> external Hermes-Agent runtime substrate -> RedCube service-safe domain entry -> executor adapter -> concrete executor -> RedCube visual-domain truth surfaces

opl_runtime_manager.layer_role: product-managed adapter/projection layer over external Hermes-Agent runtime substrate

redcube_owns: domain-agent authority across gateway -> family -> profile -> pack boundaries
must_not_claim: repo-local managed runtime pilot is the long-term runtime owner
service-safe domain entry already equals a landed end-user product entry
RedCube AI became the whole OPL
RedCube AI is an OPL-internal workflow
repo-local Hermes wrapper proves external Hermes-Agent substrate ownership

repo_verified_today: OPL Runtime Manager bridge targets external Hermes-Agent runtime substrate without owning RedCube visual-domain truth
RedCube service-safe domain entry is callable
RedCube product-entry service surface is callable
OPL handoff can federate into the same downstream product entry
direct and federated entries converge to the same downstream domain-agent entry
Codex CLI remains the default concrete executor behind executor adapter
not_landed_yet: managed web product shell on top of the landed service surface

excluded_scope: repackaging repo-local Hermes naming as upstream proof
claiming a mature end-user product shell has landed
